county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1838 from Platte Purchase. County seat: St. Joseph. Part of the Platte Purchase (1836) — last major land acquisition from Native Americans in Missouri.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in St. Joseph holds land records from 1838. St. Joseph was the starting point of the Pony Express (1860-1861) and a major outfitting point for Oregon and California Trail emigrants. The St. Joseph Museums complex holds exceptional genealogical collections. Buchanan County was part of the Platte Purchase (1836). River Heritage Museum and Pony Express Museum supplement family research. St. Joseph had significant German and Irish immigrant communities — check naturalization records at Missouri State Archives. City directories from the 1850s are valuable. Freedmen's Bureau records at NARA document post-war African American community. FamilySearch has extensive Buchanan County microfilm.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1838

source_profiles.source_name: Buchanan County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, St. Joseph
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1838 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1838)

source_profiles.source_name: Buchanan County Probate Records -- Probate Court, St. Joseph
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1838. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.repository: Recorder of Deeds, St. Joseph / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1838. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1838-1950 for Buchanan. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, slave schedules, bills of sale, manumission records, and probate records for Buchanan. Missouri was a slave state until 1865.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax records and estate inventories generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Buchanan County Area
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-removal records for multiple tribes (Osage, Missouri, Shawnee, Delaware, Sauk, Fox) in the Buchanan area. Includes Indian agency records, treaty records, removal records, and trade ledgers. Platte Purchase (1836) records at NARA-KC.

source_profiles.source_name: Buchanan County Tax Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1838. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1849 from Wayne. County seat: Poplar Bluff. Missouri Bootheel (southeastern lowlands) — cotton agriculture region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Poplar Bluff holds land records from 1849. Butler County is in the Missouri Ozarks with significant timber industry history. Butler County Historical Society in Poplar Bluff has family files and cemetery records. The Poplar Bluff Municipal Library has a genealogy section. Strong Southern settlement from Tennessee and Arkansas. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. BLM GLO records document original land entries in this public land state area. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records from 1849.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1849

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1836 from Ray. County seat: Kingston.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Kingston holds records restarting after the 1860 courthouse fire and Civil War losses. Caldwell County was a center of the 1838 Missouri-Mormon War — Far West was the LDS Church headquarters. Check LDS Church History Library in Salt Lake City for extensive Caldwell County Mormon records. Haun's Mill massacre (1838) records at Missouri State Archives. For pre-1860 records, check BLM GLO land patents and early census records (1840, 1850). Caldwell County Historical Society maintains post-fire genealogical files. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1836

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Howard, Montgomery. County seat: Fulton. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Fulton holds land records from 1820. Callaway County was nicknamed the 'Kingdom of Callaway' for its resistance to Union forces during the Civil War. Westminster College in Fulton (where Churchill gave his 'Iron Curtain' speech in 1946) has local history collections. Fulton State Hospital (1851) records may document patients and staff. Callaway County Historical Society maintains genealogical files. Strong Virginia and Kentucky settlement heritage with significant enslaved persons records pre-1865. Kingdom of Callaway Historical Society has family papers. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive for Callaway County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1820

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Benton, Laclede, Morgan, Pulaski. County seat: Camdenton. Burned county: 1902 courthouse fire destroyed most records
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Camdenton holds records restarting after the 1902 courthouse fire that destroyed most earlier records. For pre-1902 research, check Missouri State Archives, BLM GLO records, and Benton/Laclede/Morgan/Pulaski county records (parent counties). Camden County is on the Lake of the Ozarks (created 1931 by Bagnell Dam — many communities were flooded). Camden County Historical Society in Camdenton has post-fire collections. Ozark heritage with Southern settlement from Tennessee and Kentucky. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records. Check Missouri Digital Heritage for any digitized surviving records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1812 from Original district. County seat: Jackson. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803).
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Jackson holds land records from 1812 (original district). Cape Girardeau has exceptional French colonial heritage — Louis Lorimier established a trading post here in 1793 under Spanish authority. Spanish colonial land grants and court records survive at Missouri State Archives. Southeast Missouri State University in Cape Girardeau has the Kent Library Special Collections with extensive regional genealogical materials. Cape Girardeau County Archive Center holds original records. Significant German immigration in the 1830s-1850s. Trail of Tears crossing occurred at Cape Girardeau — check NARA for Cherokee removal records. Catholic parish records from the 1790s. FamilySearch has extensive microfilm for this county.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for Cape Girardeau from colonial era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Ray. County seat: Carrollton.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Carrollton holds land records from 1833. Carroll County is in the Missouri River valley with strong agricultural heritage. Carroll County Historical Society in Carrollton has genealogical files. The county was named for Charles Carroll of Carrollton, signer of the Declaration of Independence. Strong Southern settlement from Virginia and Kentucky with significant enslaved persons records in probate files. Carrollton city directories supplement county records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records including some Civil War-era loyalty oaths.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1859 from Oregon, Reynolds, Ripley, Shannon. County seat: Van Buren. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Van Buren holds records restarting after 1883 courthouse fire. For pre-1883 research, check parent counties (Oregon, Reynolds, Ripley, Shannon) and Missouri State Archives. Carter County is a remote Ozarks county on the Current River — part of the Ozark National Scenic Riverways. Very small population; limited institutional resources. Ozark heritage with Southern Appalachian settlement patterns. Carter County Historical Society has some post-fire family files. BLM GLO records document original land entries. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire records. Iron and timber industries shaped the county's economy.

county_metadata.record_losses: Order No. 11 (1863) devastation; courthouse burned; most pre-war records destroyed
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1835 from Jackson. County seat: Harrisonville. Affected by Order No. 11 (1863) — forced depopulation of western Missouri border counties during Civil War. Burned county: Order No. 11 (1863) devastation; courthouse burned; most pre-war records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Harrisonville holds records restarting after 1863 destruction. Order No. 11 devastated Cass County — virtually all structures burned and civilians expelled. This is among the most severely affected counties in Missouri. For pre-1863 records, check Missouri State Archives for surviving fragments, BLM GLO land patents, Jackson County (parent county) records, and early federal census (1850, 1860). Cass County Historical Society in Harrisonville maintains post-war resettlement records. Many families displaced to other counties or states. Quantrill's Raiders and other guerrilla bands operated here. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war restart.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1835

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Dade, St. Clair. County seat: Stockton.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Stockton holds records restarting after the 1863 courthouse burning during Civil War guerrilla warfare. For pre-1863 research, check Dade County and St. Clair County (parent counties), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO records. Cedar County Historical Society in Stockton has post-war family files. Stockton Lake area settlement history. Strong Southern heritage from Tennessee and Kentucky. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Missouri State Archives holds some surviving fragments from destroyed counties.
